text: Oh! Mr. President! I'm so glad that you reached out to me on such short notice.
I was worried this new fangled technology wouldn't work.
As you already know, time is of the essence, lest we risk nuclear war.
As such, Congress has appointed me, the youngest member,
to discuss with you our intentions and plan to remedy this disaster.
Let us strive for talks of peace!
Take it from me, young'un. Wars like these never have a solution. They just get deadlier.
So, we at Cognress urge you to discuss with Katerina Romanov, the current head of Russia.
First, collectively agree to disarm all nuclear warheads.
I'm sure Romanov will understand the need to do so if we are to achieve peace between us.
In addition, we believe that you should convince her to loosen her strict economic policies.
So, what do you say, don't you think this plan is sound?
That's great to hear from you!
If you fully agree to our proposal, I'll be happy to disconnect so you can talk to Romanov posthaste!
...Excuse me?
Oh dear, is something wrong with the plan?
I can go into more depth of our plan, if you want.
Yes? Is something wrong, Mr. President?
Alright! Good luck out there, sir. The whole country is counting on you.
Well — the whole Congress is counting on you. The country doesn't know about any of this.
If anyone leaks this, that would spell trouble, haha! Ha.
Now, how do I turn this thing off...
Is it,,, this button? No that's not it.
Hello?
Of course, Mr. President!
So, in essence, we at Congress are urging you negotiate peace with Romanov.
In these terms, we wish for you both to promise to cease the production of nuclear weapons.
On top of that, we want you to convice her to release control of her vassal states,
and to unfreeze the ecomonic embargos she has placed on our allies.
The point is: less weapons, more freedom, more America, everybody goes home happy.
Simple as that! Probably. Is that clearer, Mr. President?
I see.
Well. I won't say that doesn't sting a little.
Could you be more specific about what needs work? I have a pen here somewhere.
Actually, I have several pens. Haha! I can never find them, so I bought them in bulk
In fact, I'm sitting on a whole crate of them...Now, where was I?
Go ahead, Mr. President. I'm listening.
Unrealistic! You think the disarmament terms are unrealistic.
Then, what would you propose, sir?

links.label: I'll handle it in my own way
No need for talks. We'll strike first.

text: A better plan! Well, don't keep me in suspense, Mr. President.
I've been in Congress for seventy-one years and I can count on one hand the number of better plans I've heard.
Go ahead, sir. The floor is yours.

links.label: Any plan BUT yours will work

text: Oh! How... How dare you!
Congress has spent months creating that plan!
Do you know how much work our assistants had to do?!
I won't pretend I'm not a little frustrated with your mind games, Mr. President.
But... but you're the one with the, uh... the button. So.
I'll notify Congress at once, Mr. President.
But just remember, sir. Just because you have control over our missiles,
does NOT mean we at Congress will willingly wage a your you chose to start.
Goodbye, to you, sir.
What?
You are joking, aren't you. You must be!
Mr. President, Congress did not send me down here to authorize a first strike.
We sent me here because we finally have world peace within reach.
I urge you — strongly, emphatically, on behalf of every member of Congress: do not strike first.
Please.

links.label: Relax. It'll be over before they can retaliate.

text: You're... not joking.
Oh dear, oh dear.
Mr. President, I must formally and in the strongest possible terms object to this course of action.
On behalf of Congress, the American people, and, frankly, most of the planet.
Is there absolutely nothing I can say to change your mind?
I've had a long career, Mr. President.
Seventy-one years. Seen a lot of things. Fought in a lot of wars.
Never thought it would end like this.
Congress will never agree to this.
And I promise you, we will never allow you to wage war as if you were a tyrant.
And when word of your plans reach the American public, by God I hope you get what's coming to you.
Finally, you've come to stand with reason!
Alright. When you go into talks with Romanov, convince her of the following:
to cease the production of nuclear weapons.
to release control of her vassal states,
and to unfreeze the ecomonic embargos she has placed on our allies.
Good luck, Mr. President! Now, how do I turn this thing off?
